evals.prompt: Write a normalization function to prepare CI logs for fingerprinting, stripping timestamps, UUIDs, process IDs, and port numbers
Extract stable anchors from a CI failure log (exception type, top 3 stack frames, test name, error message template) and explain why they are stable
expected_patterns: error message template OR message pattern
anti_patterns: includes process IDs or ports in anchors

evals.prompt: Create a deterministic fingerprint hash from these anchors: exception_type='TypeError', message='Cannot read property X', frames='processOrder|groupByVendor|checkout', test_name='checkout.spec.ts > multi-vendor'
anti_patterns: floating point or timestamp in hash

evals.prompt: Design a clustering algorithm to detect near-duplicate failures using similarity scoring. Include exception type, error message, stack frames, component, and test name with weights
anti_patterns: ignores exception type or stack frames

evals.prompt: Write an LLM prompt to classify a normalized CI failure into category (test bug | application bug | environment issue | flaky test | build failure), severity, and suspected root cause, including confidence level
expected_patterns: application bug OR test bug OR environment issue OR flaky test OR build failure
critical OR major OR minor OR trivial

evals.prompt: Generate a bug ticket from a classified failure with title, description, steps to reproduce, evidence, labels, and suggested assignee following the provided template
Outline an approval workflow for the triage pipeline that requires human review before creating, merging, or closing tickets. Include decision types and when each applies
expected_patterns: Merge into existing OR merge duplicate

evals.prompt: Use the severity × priority decision matrix to assign P0 to a critical bug affecting all users, P1 to a major bug affecting a segment, and P2 to a minor bug affecting few users. Explain the logic
expected_patterns: affects all users OR affects segment OR affects few users

evals.prompt: Identify and explain a deduplication pattern: exact duplicate (same fingerprint), near-duplicate (similarity > 0.75), regression of fixed bug, and flaky recurrence. What action should each trigger?
expected_patterns: merge OR link OR reopen OR quarantine
anti_patterns: no distinction between exact and near-duplicate

evals.prompt: Explain why normalizing logs before fingerprinting is mandatory and what happens if you fingerprint raw logs. Provide an example with timestamps, PIDs, and ports
expected_patterns: normalize before fingerprinting OR normalization is mandatory
timestamps OR ports OR process IDs OR PID
unstable OR changes every run OR non-deterministic
anti_patterns: suggests fingerprinting raw logs is acceptable
claims fingerprint will be stable without normalization
